rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into the target component implementation if bindings are implementation-level; use component type scope for port-based assumptions and guarantees.
Preserve AADL architecture integrity by restricting AGREE annex content to elements explicitly defined in the AADL model structure.
generic_rules: Scope AGREE contracts to the precise component level (type or implementation) matching the origin of referenced ports or variables.
Only reference ports, data types, or properties that are explicitly modeled in the AADL architecture and declared in the component's scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax.
Implementation-level annexes must reference subcomponents or connections only after they are declared in the AADL implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not assume numeric bounds or non-negative constraints not explicitly stated in the requirement.
Do not use AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables in AGREE expressions unless mapped via explicit typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
